title: Broken Custody Map
hypothesis: Broken Custody Map tests whether archive loss can be felt through irreversible filing decisions instead of decorative particles.
dataModel: seeded custody nodes with variable edges and missing evidence tags; mutation:custody-shift
recordCountStrategy: seeded variable 13-25 records with no constant-size record array
interactionModel: pointer drag creates a recovery path; nearby nodes snap, change custody, and persist a trail; mutation mode custody-shift
stateModel: localStorage stores recovered, locked, and misfiled record ids for this draft id
visualLanguage: hard map lines, missing labels, restrained signal colors
expectedStructuralChange: Interaction must change at least one record position, class, label, count, and persisted state.
